title: matthiola bolleana subsp. morocera
intro: Matthiola bolleana subsp. morocera, also known as the sweet stock or stock flower, is a plant species belonging to the Brassicaceae family. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been traditionally used to alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis. The plant's health benefits include its ability to act as an expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, and its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain. In herbal preparations, Matthiola bolleana subsp. morocera can be used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from respiratory issues and other health concerns.
